LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_HeaderSubtitle: Indexed hostile archive for confirmed abyssal kills, tactical notes and extraction efficiency.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_LockedTag: Kill this hostile once to unseal the archive stub.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_LockedSummary: The archive has only a masked hostile signature. Record a confirmed kill to reveal the entry.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_LockedTactical: Field notes remain sealed until {0} confirmed kills.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_LockedDeep: Deep archive record remains sealed until studied status at {0} confirmed kills.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Milestone_Study: 15 kills — studied status and deep record
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_ExtractionScope: Applies only to Abyssal Protocol reward routing: horde caches, dominion payouts and supported boss-side residue/caches.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_ExtractionSummary_Next: Archive extraction bonus: +{0}% ({1} studied entries until next step)
LanguageData.ABY_BestiaryControlSummary: Threat codex: {0}/{1} discovered • {2} studied • extraction bonus +{3}%
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftImp_Summary: Cheap shock fauna pushed through unstable tears to overload firing lines and consume attention.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftImp_Tactical: Rift Imps are weak alone but dangerous in numbers. Expect them to flood doors, punish isolated shooters and screen heavier abyssal units.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftImp_Deep: The codex classifies Rift Imps as disposable breach biomass: not soldiers, but living pressure used to turn a contained engagement into a chaotic opening.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_EmberHound_Summar: Fast abyssal hounds used to reach backlines, punish kiting and force the colony to defend more than its main firing lane.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_EmberHound_Tactic: Watch for sudden lateral pressure. Ember Hounds excel at collapsing weak side rooms, chasing wounded pawns and punishing overextended ranged teams.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_EmberHound_Deep: Unlike imps, hounds are not pure disposable mass. They embody pursuit doctrine: speed, angle denial and panic spread across the colony interior.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_HexgunThrall_Tag: Disciplined line shooter of the breach.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_HexgunThrall_Summ: Armed cult-infantry that stabilizes abyssal pressure with direct fire, creating a more military breach pattern than feral swarm forms.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_HexgunThrall_Tact: Thralls are the first sign a breach has shifted from feral pressure to doctrine. Prioritize them if you need to reduce sustained ranged attrition.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_HexgunThrall_Deep: The archive notes that thralls are important psychologically: they prove the abyss does not merely spawn monsters — it fields obedient armed cadres.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChainZealot_Summa: Mid-tier assault fanatic combining pressure armor, melee threat and morale impact inside narrow approach lanes.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChainZealot_Tacti: Chain Zealots are most dangerous when imps or hounds already occupy your shooters. Kill their screen first or they will arrive on favorable terms.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChainZealot_Deep: Their presence suggests the breach has committed enough value to justify armored doctrinal melee, not just opportunistic swarm attrition.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSniper_Summar: A long-range abyssal marksman used to punish exposed specialists, turret crews and thinly protected operators.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSniper_Tactic: Treat a Rift Sniper as a force multiplier. Even a single sightline can make ordinary breach pressure dramatically more lethal.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSniper_Deep: Codex inference: the sniper role indicates the abyss can prosecute selective target denial, not merely broad aggression.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_NullPriest_Tag: Ritual support and field corruption node.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_NullPriest_Summar: A support conductor that turns a normal firefight into a ritualized pressure zone through debuffs, local distortion and allied support.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_NullPriest_Tactic: Null Priests are priority disruption targets. Leaving one alive too long usually means every other abyssal pawn becomes harder to cleanly stabilize.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_NullPriest_Deep: The archive classifies priests as proof that some abyssal hostiles do not fight for kills alone; they reshape the combat state itself.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_BreachBrute_Tag: Heavy breach ram under direct fire.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_BreachBrute_Summa: A slow armored brute intended to absorb concentrated punishment while opening room for the rest of the abyssal force.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_BreachBrute_Tacti: Brutes punish colonies that rely on a single perfect firing lane. If you cannot burst them down, be ready to rotate and delay.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_BreachBrute_Deep: The codex records breach brutes as living siege mass: a body designed less to duel and more to force the defender to spend ammunition, time and formation discipline.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_SiegeIdol_Summary: A semi-mechanical support engine that translates abyssal pressure into long-range demolition and positional punishment.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_SiegeIdol_Tactica: Once a Siege Idol is online, cover geometry becomes unstable. Static colonies will bleed value if the idol is not answered quickly.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_SiegeIdol_Deep: The archive treats idols as an important doctrinal marker: the abyss is not adapting human artillery — it is digesting siege logic into its own ritual machine form.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_Harvester_Summary: A specialist entity that becomes more dangerous as bodies accumulate, exploiting the battlefield as a resource rather than mere terrain.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_Harvester_Tactica: Do not let a Harvester operate uncontested during a messy fight. It thrives when the colony loses tempo and leaves corpses in active lanes.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_Harvester_Deep: Codex note: Harvesters demonstrate the abyssal principle that slaughter itself is material — not only a result, but a usable throughput.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_GateWarden_Summar: A compact elite guardian used to anchor important breach positions and protect boss-side assets from direct retaliation.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_GateWarden_Tactic: Wardens trade mobility for denial. Expect them near bosses, objectives and high-value breach anchors rather than in expendable waves.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_GateWarden_Deep: The archive marks wardens as personalized security, not generic mass: the abyss assigns them where a local right of access must be defended.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_WardenOfAsh_Tag: First structured mini-boss of the ashbound tier.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_WardenOfAsh_Summa: An early progression boss that combines armor, area pulse pressure and reinforcement timing into a disciplined breach test.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_WardenOfAsh_Tacti: This is the point where the abyss stops only probing and starts evaluating. Clustering and lazy melee swarms are especially punishable here.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_WardenOfAsh_Deep: The codex treats the Warden of Ash as a threshold administrator: not a supreme entity, but the first hostile expressly built to test readiness for deeper escalation.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChoirEngine_Summa: A technoritual support engine focused less on raw damage and more on turning surrounding hostiles into a synchronized pressure lattice.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChoirEngine_Tacti: The Choir Engine is a priority command target. The longer it remains online, the less any single hostile near it behaves like an isolated threat.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ChoirEngine_Deep: Archive inference: the Choir Engine proves the abyssal war-machine contains a coordination layer akin to liturgy, computation and command at once.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ReactorSaint_Summ: A late boss blending sanctified imagery, reactor pressure, defensive layering and artillery-grade punishment.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ReactorSaint_Tact: The Reactor Saint punishes static confidence. It forces the colony to answer shields, pressure zones and heavy ranged spikes in one encounter.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ReactorSaint_Deep: The codex treats this entity as evidence that the abyss can industrialize sanctity itself, turning reverence, reactor logic and violence into a single chassis.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ArchonBeast_Tag: Domain boss carrying local law of rupture.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ArchonBeast_Summa: The first true archon-class boss: a hostile that does not merely attack a map, but attempts to impose a deeper abyssal state upon it.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ArchonBeast_Tacti: Treat every phase as a state change, not a damage race. The Archon Beast is dangerous because it changes the encounter frame around itself.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_ArchonBeast_Deep: Archive classification: local administrator of rupture pressure. The visible body is only the readable front of a larger hostile authority.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSapper_Summar: A specialist hostile used to destabilize structures, openings and prepared defensive geometry rather than simply trading fire head-on.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSapper_Tactic: Rift Sappers should be intercepted early. Their value comes from changing the map on favorable terms for the rest of the breach package.
LanguageData.ABY_Bestiary_Entry_ABY_RiftSapper_Deep: The codex tags sappers as applied geometry warfare: proof that abyssal pressure can target fortification logic, not just bodies.
